title: bare-next() contract
description: Defines the observable state, action alphabet, and terminal states of the machine under test. This one document is the trace schema, the prompt contract, and the replay format.

lang.description: Source language of the code under test (controls the prompt fence). Defaults from the source file extension.

stateKeys.description: The observable-state keys, in order. The FIRST key is treated as the primary control state (used for terminal-state and coverage checks). Each entry is a bare name or { name, type }.

type.description: Human-readable type note shown in the prompt, e.g. "string or null".

initState.description: The initial state object (what init() returns). If omitted, a null-filled object over stateKeys is used.

actions.description: Map of ACTION_NAME -> { dataFields: { field: typeNote } }. An action with no payload uses an empty dataFields.

terminalStates.description: Values of the primary state key that end a scenario. Used to check each trace ends where expected.

specialRules.description: Rules that live outside the main state table (guards, rewrites, special cases). Each should be covered by >=3 windows or validate_corpus warns.

whenState.description: Optional primary-state value this rule applies in.

whenAction.description: Optional action this rule applies to.

dataDomain.description: Optional finite domain for the model checker (scripts/check.mjs): ACTION -> field -> [values...]. The checker enumerates these when iterating next(). If omitted, values are inferred from the trace corpus. Keep domains small — the state space is their product.

invariants.description: Optional path (relative to this contract) to an invariants.mjs exporting { stateInvariants:[{name,pred:(state)=>bool}], transitionInvariants:[{name,pred:(pre,action,data,post)=>bool}] }. When present, verify.mjs model-checks each spec against these rules — the bug-finding half. A sibling invariants.mjs is auto-detected.
